programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: The Prince, the Showgirl and Me
programme.desc: Documentary. Sir Laurence Olivier's personal assistant Colin Cark reveals the trials that beset The Prince and the Showgirl film with Marilyn Monroe.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take players so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. A team of three lads from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford Librarians with specialisms as diverse as Comparative Slavonic Linguistics, Classics and Theology. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random, from Goldeneye to The Kilns to Hill Top to Haworth Parsonage.

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: In the final instalment of Andrew Graham-Dixon's windswept journey through the art of the Norselands, we arrive in Sweden - home of Ikea and a tradition of brilliant furniture design stretching back to the early years of the 20th century. Sweden has made its modern democratic mission one of comfort and civilised living for the masses - but has it got there?

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 November 1999 and featuring Robbie Williams, Whitney Houston, Glamma Kid, Blur, Cliff Richard, Texas and Wamdue Project.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1981 and featuring The Polecats, UB40, Hazel O'Connor, Coast to Coast, Adam & the Ants and Kim Carnes, plus a dance performance from Legs & Co.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: John Peel and Janice Long present this pop chart programme, first broadcast on 29 May 1986. Featuring Doctor and the Medics, Robert Palmer, The Real Thing, Tears for Fears, Pete Wylie, Spitting Image and Peter Gabriel.

programme.title: Miles Davis: Birth of the Cool
programme.desc: Miles Davis - horn player, bandleader, innovator. Elegant, intellectual, vain. Callous, conflicted, controversial. Magnificent, mercurial. Genius. The very embodiment of cool. The man with a sound so beautiful it could break your heart. The central theme of Miles Davis's life was his restless determination to break boundaries and live life on his own terms. It made him a star. It also made him incredibly difficult to live with for the people who loved him most. Again and again, in music and in life, Miles broke with convention - and when he thought his work came to represent a new convention, he changed it again. Miles's bold disregard for tradition, his clarity of vision, his relentless drive and constant thirst for new experiences made him an inspiring collaborator to fellow musicians and a cultural icon to generations of listeners. It made him an innovator in music - from bebop to cool jazz, modern quintets, orchestral music, jazz fusion, rock ‘n’ roll and even hip-hop. Featuring never-before-seen archival footage, studio outtakes and rare photos, this film tells the story of a truly singular talent and unpacks the music and the myth of the man behind the horn.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: Alan Yentob shares the fascinating story of how the 1974 broadcast of the great Ella Fitzgerald singing in Ronnie Scott’s nightclub came about. In a tale of tenacity combined with new camera technology, Alan recounts how, as a young TV producer, he managed to persuade Ella’s manager to let the BBC film Ella in full flight at London’s home of jazz, capturing forever what is now considered to be a performance of true musical significance.

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald's Other Show
programme.desc: Ella Fitzgerald in performance at Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club in 1974.

programme.title: Cleo Laine at the BBC
programme.desc: A special celebration of Britain’s First Lady of Jazz’s finest moments on the BBC to coincide with her 95th birthday. Cleo Laine became a star after joining The John Dankworth Seven in the 1950s. Internationally renowned for her mastery of the ‘scat’ singing style, and with a voice that has been described as ‘one of the richest contraltos in recording history’, Cleo has now enjoyed over six decades of success and acclaim here and across the world. These are the highlights of her extraordinary career, with classic songs like Send in the Clowns and I Love You, Porgy, and duets with John Williams, Dudley Moore, Tony Bennett, and of course, her musical partner and fellow jazz legend Johnny Dankworth.

programme.title: Jazz 625 at the BBC
programme.desc: Programme celebrating the archive of this legendary BBC jazz concert show from the mid 1960s, so titled because the newly launched BBC 2 was broadcasting on 625-UHF lines (the HD of the time) unlike the 405-VHF of BBC 1 and ITV. It features classic performances from legends such as Duke Ellington, Dave Brubeck and Dizzy Gillespie and The Julian 'Cannonball' Adderley Sextet, alongside rarely seen sessions from the likes of Wes Montgomery, Marian McPartland, Chris Barber's Jazz Band and Willie 'The Lion' Smith.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 November 1999 and featuring Geri Halliwell, Travis, Ricky Martin, A1, Will Smith, Stereophonics, Prince and Robbie Williams.

programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 November 1999 and featuring Robbie Williams, Whitney Houston, Glamma Kid, Blur, Cliff Richard, Texas and Wamdue Project.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, filmed in 1977 from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Frankie Vaughan, Mike and Bernie Winters, Maryetta Midgley, Zena Millar and members of the Players Theatre, London.

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Dusty Springfield's 1960s variety show, first broadcast on 15 August 1967, with the voices of Madeline Bell, Leslie Duncan and Maggie Stredder. Her special guests are Alf Garnett aka actor Warren Mitchell and actor and writer Ken Campbell.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Drama series about a 60-year-old private detective. A resident of a retirement home calls on Hetty's help.

programme.title: Ghost Trail
programme.desc: Hamid joins a secret group tracking Syrian regime leaders on the run. His mission takes him to France, pursuing his former torturer for a fateful confrontation. In this manhunt thriller inspired by true events, the past and present collide, exploring themes of justice and redemption. In French with English subtitles.

programme.title: This Is Joan Collins
programme.desc: A feature-length documentary on the life of one of the last surviving actresses from the golden age of Hollywood – Joan Collins. This epic film is told from the ringside as Joan narrates her rollercoaster life story with her inimitable wit and verve. A worldwide television phenomenon with her decade-defining role in Dynasty, Collins shares her extraordinary archive and never before seen home movie footage, giving an intimate glimpse into one of the world’s most iconic figures. Against a backdrop of Collins’s own narration, her story showcases the extraordinary life of a woman who has lived through the glitz, the glamour and the enduring moments of Hollywood history, and survived it all with panache.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an irrepressible snob. Hyacinth is forced to intervene when Daddy overspends in a toy department, dresses up as a spaceman and proceeds to go on the rampage. Unless the damage is paid for, the owners threaten to apprehend him as a security risk.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: With Frank's wedding impending, Timothy sets his cap at bridesmaid Liz. Will our hero get his head out of the railings in time? Will he forget the ring? And if he gets married, will Mother come on the honeymoon?

programme.title: The Good Old Days

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a programme that follows several thousand pioneering young penguins as they invade Boulders Beach, a busy resort in Cape Town, in an attempt to establish a new colony amid the striped umbrellas and sun loungers.

programme.title: Top Hat
programme.desc: In the quintessential Astaire and Rogers musical, an American dancer staying at a hotel in London falls in love with the guest staying in the room below. However, she gets her wires crossed, convinces herself that the dancer is already hitched and hotfoots it off to Venice. Classic numbers include Cheek to Cheek and Top Hat. This is Fred and Ginger's fourth pairing, but the first with a screenplay written specifically for them.

programme.title: Poems in Their Place: Thomas Hardy
programme.desc: Dr Robert Gittings explores the relationship between poems and the places they were written. He visits Sturminster Newton, where Thomas Hardy enjoyed two very happy years.

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: With a passion for art that is rivalled only by travel, Michael Palin combines both in a European journey to discover more about Vilhelm Hammershoi, an enigmatic Danish artist that has fascinated him for years. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, Holland and Copenhagen.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: The first real ballet school was founded by King Louis XIV of France in 1669. From Louis's great palace at Versailles, Margot Fonteyn tells the story of Louis's own love of dancing and how it led from the courtly dances of 17th-century France to the worldwide phenomenon of ballet that we know today. At Drottningholm in Sweden, she visits the Court Theatre and sees ballet performed in the original settings and under the original conditions.

programme.title: Dance Rebels: A Story of Modern Dance
programme.desc: Documentary which tells the compelling story of the mavericks like Isadora Duncan, Martha Graham, Merce Cunningham and Pina Bausch, whose radical ideas created modern dance in the 20th century. With historical archive and the first-hand experiences of student dancers from the Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance, it shows the ideas that challenged audiences and changed dance forever. Also, the biggest stars in dance explain what inspired them to create their own groundbreaking choreography. With contributions from Michael Clark, William Forsythe, Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker, Wayne McGregor, Akram Khan, Boris Charmatz and Lea Anderson.

programme.title: Michael Palin and the Mystery of Hammershoi
programme.desc: With a passion for art that is rivalled only by travel, Michael Palin combines both in a European journey to discover more about Vilhelm Hammershoi, an enigmatic Danish artist that has fascinated him for years. Curious to see more of Hammershoi's paintings and discover what kind of life the artist lived, Michael searches for clues in London, Holland and Copenhagen.

programme.desc: Shiona McCallum visits Fifa HQ in Zurich to see some of the new tech being used in the World Cup. programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Rachel gets into the swing of a cosmopolitan Riviera town, uncovering foodie gems in Nice's charming back streets. She meets some hip young French chefs cooking with bohemian flair at a small local bistro that prides itself on fresh seasonal produce with a cool laid-back vibe. She also visits a socca bar where she learns about the giant chickpea crépe, a speciality of the region. Back in London, Rachel cooks little socca cakes with ratatouille, confit cod with rainbow chard gratin and mini orange trifles with candied carrots.

The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he's encountered in the worst places. In this film, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present, the world has real cause for hope.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: The Smuggling Business: Undercover
programme.desc: Over the past two years, tens of thousands of migrants have crossed the English Channel in small boats from northern France, despite repeated promises by successive governments to stop the trade. This undercover BBC investigation reveals how people smuggling gangs are operating in plain sight inside a migrant camp in Dunkirk. It exposes a highly organised criminal marketplace driven by fierce competition between rival groups, who enforce control over the settlements through fear, violence and intimidation. The film examines efforts by UK authorities to disrupt and dismantle the criminal networks profiting from the crossings, and questions whether those measures are keeping pace with the scale and sophistication of the operation. BBC investigative reporter Josie Hannett also travels to Crowborough, where opposition to a new asylum seeker accommodation site has turned the town into a flashpoint in the national immigration debate. She asks whether current government policy is likely to deter future arrivals, or whether the UK is preparing for a future that will require many more such sites across the south east

programme.title: Discovering the World's Table
programme.desc: In Discovering the World's Table, food writer Nick Kwek embarks on a culinary journey in Poland. He visits two of the country's most vibrant cities Warsaw and Poznan to explore how history and culture inspire the food scene. Starting in Warsaw, Nick meets Olga Bradowska, a local food journalist, to learn about the enduring importance of milk bars in Polish cuisine. They are Polish cafeterias that used to provide low-cost, government-subsidised food during the Communist era. Next, Nick travels to the outskirts of the city to Majlert's farm, a family-run business since the 18th century. He joins renowned chef Marcin Przybysz to gather asparagus for his restaurant, which serves dishes based on recipes from prewar cook books. Poznan has a thriving food scene, from Michelin-starred restaurants to trendy cafes. The city offers a mix of traditional Polish cuisine and modern gastronomic trends. Nick uncovers the secrets behind the world's only legally protected croissant, St Martin's croissant, dating back to 1891. He samples the city's famous potatoes and discovers why a smoked beer is considered 'the champagne of Poland'.

programme.desc: John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses the possibility of the US forcing regime change in Cuba, examines the effect of the war in Ukraine on Vladimir Putin's standing in Russia and looks at the increasing popularity of prediction markets.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: - Morning Live is BBC One’s flagship daytime programme, bringing you the best advice from familiar faces across the BBC, original journalism and investigations, and a sparkle of Strictly magic

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: A man whose home address is hijacked by a fraudster using the name Michael Jackson receives traffic fines and a visit from a bailiff but says the authorities won’t help. Also on the programme, how a leading airline’s loyalty points scheme is being targeted by thieves.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin meets a big fan of the show in Hampshire but is disappointed to hear he’s ignored the golden rules of buying at auction. In Kent, we see the conclusion of a period restoration that Jacqui first saw in early 2023. Dion’s taking in the scenery in beautiful north Wales.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion visits a pub frozen in time, where last orders were called in the 1960s and everything was left untouched. In the Devon town of Buckfastleigh, the Valiant Soldier was an inn for more than two centuries and is now a museum, giving visitors a glimpse into the past. Inside the recreated interior, Christina is told about the pub’s redoubtable landlady and the custom back in the 60s of refusing service to women in the bar. In Hele, near Exeter, two teams of shoppers rush around an antiques centre with their experts, hoping to bag profitable bargains. On the red team are dad and daughter Ade and Mel, helped by Ben Cooper, and for the blues, it’s friends Martin and Mark, aided by Gary Pe. With 60 minutes on the clock, the blue team’s first stop is a shining suit of armour. Across the centre, Mel gazes into a £200 crystal ball – does it foretell a win for the reds? In Chippenham, auctioneer Tim Weeks confidently predicts a few profits for the teams, but who will go home the victor?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: The Oakley winery 'family' is shaken by a tremor and strained relationships. Daisy is stressed, Louis is leaving, while Nic and Vic make a request. Return of the earthy New Zealand comedy drama.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Nic suffers distress on a hike, but Dr David helps and heartens Daisy. Gus and Griffin brave Morag’s guesthouse. Struck-off Louis gets legal.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Now that her sons have fledged the nest, today’s buyer wants a new lease of life in a rural home that has business potential. She’s set her sights on either Devon or Dorset, where Briony May Williams has some wonderful property options to show her. Briony also meets another entrepreneurial couple who’ve converted an old railway carriage into a fine dining destination.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents as a team of celebrity sport stars - former England rugby player Ugo Monye, tennis supremo Judy Murray, goalkeeper David James and Scottish football legend Pat Nevin - take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

With no photos and only scraps of memory to guide her, miniaturist Abi Trotman faces one of her toughest builds yet. Can she capture the warmth, loss and love bound up in Sandra’s most precious place?

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The 1985 Black Ball Final made snooker legend Dennis Taylor a household name, and there's nowhere that celebrates this giant of sport more than his native Tyrone. Anna visits him at the snooker hall where it all began. Dennis can still remember the soda bread his grandmother used to make over an open fire. Can Anna recreate this childhood recipe? The pair then join fishermen on the largest lake in the UK to celebrate one of Ireland's most unique delicacies - the Lough Neagh eel.

One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will half their money. programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre returns with his Big Show, filmed at London's prestigious Theatre Royal Drury Lane. programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: It’s Jacob’s birthday, and everyone notices there’s something up with Zoe. She punishes Dan as she focuses on Solomon’s big career break. Dan’s romantic trip might offer hope, but it triggers deeply buried memories for Zoe. Did she choose this island for a reason? Jacob is trying to hide his age from Will, not helped by Will’s Gen Z mates gatecrashing their night out at a local taverna. When Jess goes on the attack to save her own skin, Zoe is left with nowhere to go but out.

programme.desc: Highlights of the 2026 UEFA Champions League final between PSG and Arsenal in Budapest. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin wants to run for the hills because of a tricky legal issue in Devon. Jacqui is in Eastbourne, where work is already under way at an end-of-terrace. And Dion goes to Northampton, where a smelly cellar is hard to cope with!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles is in Peterborough for one of the largest antiques fairs in the UK. He’s joined by Bargain Hunt regular Colin Young, who has some top tips for porcelain collectors. Experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell guide the red and blue teams around the fair, but who will go home empty-handed and who is making a big score?

programme.title: Songs of Praise

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television programmes. Whether you’ve seen something you’ve loved or loathed on the BBC, get in touch with your point of view. Email: pov@bbc.co.uk X: @bbcPoV Facebook: Search for BBC Points of View

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A pair of artists look to Alistair Appleton to help them find an inspiring rural retreat in Somerset where they can further indulge their creativity. Alistair also meets a young couple who moved to the countryside in search of the good life and never looked back, despite ups and downs along the way.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Amazon, where extraordinary animal behaviours reveal the rainforest’s hidden stories. In southern Brazil, over a million turtle hatchlings make a perilous dash to the river as black caiman prey on them – a newly filmed behaviour. In Venezuela, a young harpy eagle that has been watched from its earliest days endures intense storms while awaiting its father’s return with food, providing new understanding of life high in the canopy. Also in Brazil, giant river otters hunt together in a coordinated effort, displaying unique social bonds as they corral fish in the shallows. Army ants in Ecuador break down their bivouac and march through the forest with a level of precision and cooperation seldom seen. In the Pantanal, caiman engage in an elaborate courtship display, their movements sending ripples across the water – a striking look at an ancient ritual.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The frozen north, where survival means constantly adapting to an unforgiving landscape. In Hudson Bay, a rare encounter between the region's top predators unfolds as wolves and polar bears clash over a single carcass – a behaviour never caught on camera until now. Under the glow of the northern lights, snowshoe hares use their large feet to escape lynx in the vast boreal forest. In Alberta, sharp-tailed grouse put on a show like never before, practising their courtship dances under car headlights. Off Alaska’s coast, male walruses engage in curious vocal displays, singing together under the sunset. In summer, millions of salmon begin their epic journey upstream, while newborn caribou navigate dangerous river crossings on their first migratory trek. Life here is a series of bold moves and dramatic encounters, in a place where the stakes are always high.

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Animated sequel following the grumpy ogre and his bride as they head for the land of Far Far Away to meet her parents. As Shrek contends with his new in-laws, the wicked Fairy Godmother sets in motion a dastardly scheme to scupper his marriage to Fiona. And will any of them survive at the hands of dandy assassin Puss in Boots? Featuring the voices of Mike Myers (Shrek), Eddie Murphy (Donkey), Cameron Diaz (Princess Fiona), Antonio Banderas (Puss in Boots), Julie Andrews (Queen), John Cleese (King), Rupert Everett (Prince Charming), Jennifer Saunders (Fairy Godmother), Aron Warner (Wolf), Kelly Asbury (Page/Elf/Nobleman/Nobleman's Son), Cody Cameron (Pinocchio/Three Pigs) and Conrad Vernon (Gingerbread Man/Cedric/Announcer/Muffin Man).

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Adam Henson are in Lisburn, Northern Ireland, for the Balmoral Show, the region’s largest agricultural and food event, welcoming more than 100,000 visitors each year. As the show celebrates its 157th year, Sean and Adam meet the people who make it such a vital gathering for the region’s farming community and explore how events like this are crucial to their future. Adam explores heritage crafts that community members are determined to preserve, and he joins families showing their prize livestock. Sean takes charge of the stopwatch at the tractor-handling competition and gets hands-on with traditional local produce that is valuable to the region’s economy.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A special episode commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings in June 1944, filmed in Normandy and Portsmouth, and featuring testimony from those who were there.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: When a runner overdoses in an actor’s trailer, Janie and John are called to investigate. On the set of an epic Welsh TV drama, the team interview the crew to solve the crime.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the devastating defeat at Wembley, Southgate’s guilt weighs heavily on his conscience. When the fans then turn on Southgate, accusing him of prioritising politics over performance, he begins to doubt himself ahead of a controversial World Cup in Qatar.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Nice Guys
programme.desc: In 1970s Los Angeles, a mismatched pair of private eyes investigate a missing girl and the mysterious death of a porn star. Comedy starring Ryan Gosling and Russell Crowe.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police concerns grow about the mental state of two missing teens - 16-year-old Larissa has disappeared after an argument with her mum, and 18-year-old Bobby may be a suicide risk.

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni explores the life of Sean 'Diddy' Combs, who has been jailed on prostitution-related charges She meets some of his inner circle to find out more about him.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murders of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: The Fast and the Farmer-ish
programme.desc: It’s all tension and tactics in this hotly contested semi-final. With tortuous challenges ahead, teams are focused on bagging maximum points to get to the final and avoid defeat.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: 24 Hours with Ella Toone
programme.desc: Unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her biggest fan, her dad.

programme.title: Shrek
programme.desc: Animated comedy. In a land of living fairy tales and nursery rhymes, grumpy ogre Shrek, along with the loudmouthed Donkey, joins beautiful but wilful Princess Fiona in tackling Lord Farquaad's evil schemes to exile the exotic from his lands and wed the unwilling princess.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team look forward to welcoming a new recruit into their midst, whilst Mervin can’t wait to get off the island. But before he catches his flight, he becomes embroiled in the murder of a young man who’s found in a ravine. The case becomes more compelling when they find the victim has left a mysterious message.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul is lurking in the shadows, attempting to fix the competition…

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie’s been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: After solving the Andie Bell case, Pip is forced to confront the consequences of what her investigation exposed. It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip. In an attempt to rebuild her relationship with Cara, Pip releases a podcast to tell the world the real story. The only person yet to face justice is Max Hastings, and as his trial approaches, Pip is determined to help his victims find closure. However, the prosecution’s case begins to falter when Becca Bell withdraws from testifying. Unwilling to accept this, Pip musters the courage to visit Becca in prison with the hope of convincing her that giving evidence still matters. Pip wants to focus on supporting Ravi at the upcoming memorial for his brother, but when a mysterious note promising information on Max appears in her locker, Pip must make a difficult decision. Little Kilton comes together to say goodbye to both Andie Bell and Sal Singh. The end of one dark chapter gives way to another, as Cara finds herself in a dangerous situation, and Jamie Reynolds, Connor’s brother and a key witness in Max’s trial, disappears without warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie’s recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Radio 1’s Sian Eleri begins an investigation into a seemingly ordinary farmhouse in north Wales that was once known as the most haunted house in Britain.

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian is frustrated that she still hasn’t been able to contact the Gower family who lived at the farmhouse – David, a former headmaster, his wife Rose-Mary and their son John-Paul. But there is a breakthrough of another kind when the current owner of Penyffordd Farm gets in touch and invites her over to see the house she’s heard so much about. Sian is welcomed in by Michael, who bought the house in Flintshire from the Gowers despite stories that it was haunted. He never believed their accounts, but he has heard rumours of the gravestone of a teenage girl called Jane Jones, which he suspects might still be on the property somewhere. The archive from Dr Daniels suggests that moving this gravestone was believed to be the precursor for all the strange happenings in the house, so it's the next lead for Sian to chase. Ghost or not, is there any historical evidence to suggest there really is a girl buried at Penyffordd Farm – and if so, why would she be buried in the garden? Sian tracks down a genealogist to see if there’s any truth to the story and finally gets an opportunity to speak to a member of the Gower family…

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian Eleri’s investigation into the haunting of Penyffordd Farm continues as multiple witnesses claim to have seen a hooded figure. Could they all have imagined the same thing?

programme.title: Paranormal
programme.desc: Sian's investigation into an allegedly haunted house in north Wales concludes. Can she track down the last family members and solve whether this was paranormal or all a hoax?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his glorious comeback. In Tokyo 2020, Darren Silverstone was the Paras GB poster boy. (He was on yoghurts, too.) He cruised to the final and charmed the nation along the way with his signature dance move, the dazzle. But as he took that final bend ready to take gold, Silverstone lost his head and celebrated too soon - and in the process, became a national figure of fun. His subsequent journey in the public eye brought approval and consternation in equal measure: from those unforgettably self-deprecating betting adverts to his turn on The Masked Singer as Breadbin. Darren’s best friend and pilot rider in Tokyo, little Paul Potter, was never seen again in the public eye. The two haven’t spoken since. And now, Silverstone’s back. Ready to take to the tandem at Paris 2024. But is he ready? Who will he be riding with? This is I Am Darren.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul is lurking in the shadows, attempting to fix the competition…

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie’s been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: In the near future, the Doctor and Clara find themselves on a space shuttle making a suicide mission to the moon. Crash-landing on the lunar surface, they find a mining base full of corpses, vicious spider-like creatures poised to attack, and a terrible dilemma. When Clara turns to the Doctor for help, she gets the shock of her life.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara are on the most beautiful train in history, speeding among the stars of the future. But a deadly creature is stalking the passengers. Once you see the horrifying mummy, you only have 66 seconds to live. No exceptions, no reprieve. As the Doctor races against the clock, Clara sees him at his deadliest and most ruthless. Will he work out how to defeat the mummy? Start the clock!

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Tilly's fiancee Rupert is on army leave and their wedding plans are in full swing. Miranda is set up with Rupert's ghastly best man Ping Pong Charlie at the same time as Stevie and Clive set her up with a 'special someone' else. Her date with the 'special someone' gives Miranda a new-found confidence that Ping Pong Charlie and Rupert find irresistible. How will she shake off Ping Pong Charlie? Should she tell Tilly about Rupert's advances, and more importantly, how long will the 'special someone' wait for a kiss?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda and Stevie compete to be best friends with the new waitress at the restaurant, Tamara. The trouble is that Tamara is 22, and they both find keeping up with an energetic 20-something surprisingly tiring. Their age becomes an issue after an art class, followed by all-night karaoke, followed by a swimming experience that puts Miranda face-to-face with her nudity issues. With Miranda and Stevie united, will they keep up with Tamara, or will they just bung their slippers on, get a hot chocolate and settle down in front of Midsomer Murders?

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There's a storm blowing in Finglas, and it soon claims its first victim when the pipes burst in Winnie and Sharon's house. Thank goodness for Mrs Brown, who is on hand to offer a roof over their heads while repairs are carried out. It's not long before there's a storm brewing inside the Brown household too. Agnes and Cathy discover that living with best friends isn't the picnic they'd imagined. Agnes has yet more on her plate when she receives news of the death of a neighbour she never really knew - or even liked - and is asked by Father Damian to do her eulogy. And there is Bono's thirteenth birthday to host. It might all seem too much for Agnes, and then Buster drops a bombshell.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There’s bad news for Winnie when Agnes discovers Peggy Piper the Pernod poisoner is out on appeal and could be coming back to Finglas. This could mean Winnie has to give evidence against her again as she was the one who saw Peggy poison her husband. Question is, is Peggy guilty or innocent? There's only one way of knowing for sure - and that's by meeting Peggy herself. Meanwhile, Buster loses the expensive tank costume Dermot’s hired for their latest promotion and must find it before it's too late. Sue Vincent guest stars.

programme.title: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret.
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Margaret is uprooted from her life in New York City for the suburbs of New Jersey, going through the messy and tumultuous throes of puberty with new friends in a new school. She relies on her mother, Barbara, who is also struggling to adjust to life outside the big city, and her adoring grandmother, Sylvia, who isn’t happy they moved away and likes to remind them every chance she gets. Based on Judy Blume’s classic novel.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul is lurking in the shadows, attempting to fix the competition…

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie’s been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Now that Gaz and Donna are divorced, Gaz decides the time has come to propose to Janet. Scared that she is losing all her friends, in particular Gaz, Donna insists on helping him organise the proposal, much to the disapproval of Tim who worries she's holding onto the past and not moving on. Gaz and Donna enjoy spending time together, possibly too much, and it conjures up old feelings. Wesley gives Janet a job on the market stall and teaches her the trade, but she struggles as she suffers increasingly from a stomach ache. When the pain gets so bad and she is rushed to hospital, she asks Wesley to phone Gaz, but he is nowhere to be found.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet arrives back from hospital with Wesley and Tim, minus an appendix, and there is no sign of Gaz. Janet catches him out when he comes creeping back into the house later on. When he finds out that she has spent the night with Wesley holding her hand he blurts out that he has slept with Donna, so Janet throws him out. Louise tells Tim she is desperate for a boyfriend, just as ex-boyband member Scott enters the pub. He tries to woo her, but she is convinced there must be something wrong with him. There is, but it's not what she thinks. Gaz is torn between the two girls and although he decides to stay with Janet for the sake of Corinthian, when he goes to tell Donna he realises he loves her too and can't be without her. Janet and Wesley go to the flat only to discover them in bed together once more. They demand he makes a choice between Janet and Donna.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassess his life and values.

When Clara turns to the Doctor for help, she gets the shock of her life.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max confronts the Mitchells about his suspicions, Gina gives Eddie the cold shoulder, and Zack gets some unwelcome news.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, and Eddie and Harry clash.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s the day of Vicki and Ross’s wedding, but will it go ahead? Priya gets a shock.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at Vicki and Ross’s wedding, and Denise receives some news.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: After solving the Andie Bell case, Pip is forced to confront the consequences of what her investigation exposed. It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip. In an attempt to rebuild her relationship with Cara, Pip releases a podcast to tell the world the real story. The only person yet to face justice is Max Hastings, and as his trial approaches, Pip is determined to help his victims find closure. However, the prosecution’s case begins to falter when Becca Bell withdraws from testifying. Unwilling to accept this, Pip musters the courage to visit Becca in prison with the hope of convincing her that giving evidence still matters. Pip wants to focus on supporting Ravi at the upcoming memorial for his brother, but when a mysterious note promising information on Max appears in her locker, Pip must make a difficult decision. Little Kilton comes together to say goodbye to both Andie Bell and Sal Singh. The end of one dark chapter gives way to another, as Cara finds herself in a dangerous situation, and Jamie Reynolds, Connor’s brother and a key witness in Max’s trial, disappears without warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie’s recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Arthur the King
programme.desc: Over the course of ten days and 435 miles, an unbreakable bond is forged between pro adventure racer Michael Light and a scrappy street dog companion dubbed Arthur. Desperate for one last chance to win, Michael convinces a sponsor to back him and a team of athletes for the Adventure Racing World Championship in the Dominican Republic. As the team is pushed to their outer limits of endurance in the race, Arthur redefines what victory, loyalty and friendship truly mean. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain’s most maligned ethnic minorities.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the final five designers head to Leeds to transform studio rental apartments. They must cleverly divide open-plan spaces and inject character into show apartments to entice potential renters. Former Interior Design Masters winner Lynsey Ford joins Michelle to critique the designs, and they are blown away by the dramatically different results.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen joins response officers Anna and Perri as they attend a 999 call made from a property in Belfast. A man has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes during a domestic dispute. At risk to their own safety, the officers enter the property to find the suspect upstairs, reportedly self-harming. There are reports he has strangled a dog. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has left a pedestrian badly injured. As soon as they arrive, a scared and tearful woman approaches. She thinks the man lying in the middle of the road, being worked on by paramedics, is her partner. It’s down to Tom to deliver a message the woman will never forget. Later, adrenaline levels spike as Stephen and the team race to reports of an angry crowd surrounding the home of a convicted sex offender. As the tension rises, Stephen engages with the increasingly irate crowd, while Nigel formulates a plan to get the sex offender out of his house to safety.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But can she keep pretending when her husband Dan’s dark secret threatens to ruin everything? A hedonistic first night party is the release they all need. But it sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play. We are in Greece, after all, land of the gods.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion’s in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his travels in Budapest, Michael Portillo begins in the small town of Huvösvölgy, in the hills above the city, at a surprising railway run almost entirely by children, dating back to the communist era. All of the staff, except for the train drivers, are volunteers aged ten to 14. Back in the city, he visits the former home of Béla Bartók, one of Hungary’s most famous pianists and composers, and learns how the country’s folk music inspired his work. Next, Michael heads across the Danube to the headquarters of the low-cost airline Wizz Air, one of the country’s post-communist success stories. At the pilot academy, he endures a stressful emergency landing and tries his hand at flying a plane.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain’s great houses – and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provides support as well as trained assistance dogs to help those living with deafness or hearing loss in the UK.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: The unique quiz show where if a player gives an Impossible answer, they are immediately eliminated. Which of the 24 contestants will face the £10,000 question today?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another heat of the classic quiz where contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are the British Industrial Revolution, the films of Christopher Guest, Charles Rennie Mackintosh and Succession.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: The county of Yorkshire is the setting for Christine Walkden's balloon tour of Britain. At Scampston Hall we discover two superstar garden designers and meet young gardener Nathan, who is just starting out. At York Gate Garden, Christine catches up with an old friend and we discover the Yorkshireman who was the father of aviation.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: After the drought, the rains have arrived, but has baby elephant Breeze survived? As hundreds of elephants descend on the reserve for the breeding season, the research team hit their busiest time of year. With the help of new technology, Saba Douglas-Hamilton tracks down the biggest land animals on earth - the mighty bull elephants. But even Saba is keeping her distance when Rommel, a dangerous bull who once flattened a car and tried to kill two of the team's researchers, arrives on the scene. Outside the reserve, there is a shocking development when Onesmas Kahindi investigates two mysterious deaths - could poachers be targeting the local elephants? When field researcher David Daballen finds a baby elephant in desperate trouble, he and the team must mount a life-or-death operation to save her.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the experts visit Chichester Cathedral. Finds include a fine cow creamer, a rare 18th-century games purse and a valuable necklace which was found in an attic.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Crowcombe Court in West Somerset. The grand house was built in 1739 as a statement of wealth and a place to entertain. Antiques experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant uncover some promotional sausage plates that were designed to promote the wares of an exuberant Victorian butcher. Paul also rides on the footplate of a steam locomotive on West Somerset Railway.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Before boarding his train at Budapest’s historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth. Heading south, his next destination is the country’s third city of Szeged, close to the Serbian and Romanian borders. In the city’s historic centre, he finds the magnificent Great Synagogue, built in 1902, which reflects the size and prominence of Szeged’s Jewish population at that time. Just outside the city, on the banks of the River Tisza, Michael visits a region known for producing Hungary’s favourite spice, paprika, from ground red peppers. At a fourth-generation family farm, he tries a freshly picked pepper and joins the family for a hearty paprika stew.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The wait is over: the search for Northern Ireland’s greatest garden is almost at an end. Five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting these five truly incredible gardens to refresh their memories, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmount’s walled garden in County Antrim to reveal which one of the five will be named greatest garden.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Some more unusual methods of growing are demonstrated at Beechgrove Garden. Ruth Vichos attempts to produce a mushroom crop and there’s a report on some of Scotland’s best bonsai, with everything you need to know to start that form of cultivation. Brian Cunningham is in the Seaside Garden at Beechgrove refreshing the planting with varieties that can withstand a coastal climate and Brian turns conifers into sculptures with topiary. Plus the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With summer just around the corner, Monty is bringing bold colour to the garden, planting gingers and cannas, setting out tomatoes and sowing wallflowers straight into the soil. From her Devon garden, Carol Klein is exploring the latest plant science, uncovering what is really going on inside a seed the moment it is sown, and how this pioneering knowledge can transform the way people garden. Rekha Mistry is also on a mission to grow big in a small space, using only pots on her patio. In Oxfordshire, a garden designed for both beauty and biodiversity shows how plants can support wildlife as well as people. And a viewer in rural France shares a breathtaking, naturalistic plot that blends seamlessly with the surrounding landscape.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a windswept island chapel battered by the North Sea to a quiet Welsh farmhouse that shaped the future of a language, this episode travels to two remote National Trust sites whose powerful stories have inspired centuries of pilgrims, revealing the delicate conservation work protecting their remarkable stories.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul, Bob and Ted travel a short distance to Thornwood Springs Fishery in Essex, a complex of three lakes on the edges of Epping Forest, in search of a big rudd - although there's also the chance of a trout in the waters. Later, they pull up at a churchyard, and Bob reveals they are ‘champing’ – camping overnight in a church, in this case St Nicholas’s in the village of Berden.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Lucy Hockings.

programme.title: Love & Mercy
programme.desc: Biographical drama. At the height of success with his band The Beach Boys, musical genius Brian Wilson begins to suffer from mental illness, which is not helped by his experiments with LSD. A decade later, Wilson is in the grip of shady psychotherapist Eugene Landy, whose extreme treatment is causing further deterioration, until the day he decides he wants a new car.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week is coming to an end, but not before a few more cooks have to say goodbye. Ten contestants remain, and there is only room in the semi-finals for eight.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The competition intensifies as the celebrity trio, EastEnders star Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, return for day 2. Vernon Kay keeps order as the quiz master, while sidekick Paul Gorton takes the contestants on a tour of today’s destination, Dudley. The race to the weekly finale is well and truly on.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night’s betrayal didn’t mean anything. But Dan’s behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn’t know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piripenguins face a few tricky problems, but they discover that swapping items and working together can provide clever solutions, turning a regular day into an extraordinary one.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A strange rock far away from the ship breaks D.O.R.I.S.’s satnav. Luckily, Big Lizard’s on hand to teach Cosima how to find her way around without technology.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy promises to go camping with the ducklings, but he wants to watch football instead. During half-time, Weasy attempts to finish camping as quickly as possible.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Twinkle feels she is being taken for granted by the other heroes, so she joins the Fairy Squad when they arrive.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to pick coconuts, but the Lemmings find a magic conch.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job as a baker, Frog realises he can get free treats! However, a mix-up with the dough causes a birthday cake to grow out of control!

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Trainers put an intriguing new device into action at an Exceed testing centre.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe become bus tour guides, except they must say and do as the other says. With Holly H and Radio 1’s Rickie joining as passengers, what could go wrong?

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Diamond, Cyclone, Nitro and Fire go head-to-head dishing out the dares in a restaurant. Will a robot, rock star, broccoli or water over the head cause a tap out?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Blue Peter goes access all areas at Radio 1's Big Weekend with our lucky competition winner! Joel, Abby and Shini try DIY festival make-up looks, and The Adventures of Rap Kid by MC Grammar is the latest read in the Blue Peter Book Club.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With summer just around the corner, Monty is bringing bold colour to the garden, planting gingers and cannas, setting out tomatoes and sowing wallflowers straight into the soil. From her Devon garden, Carol Klein is exploring the latest plant science, uncovering what is really going on inside a seed the moment it is sown, and how this pioneering knowledge can transform the way people garden. Rekha Mistry is also on a mission to grow big in a small space, using only pots on her patio. In Oxfordshire, a garden designed for both beauty and biodiversity shows how plants can support wildlife as well as people. And a viewer in rural France shares a breathtaking, naturalistic plot that blends seamlessly with the surrounding landscape.

programme.title: Jailhouse Rock
programme.desc: Sentenced to jail for manslaughter, Vince Everett is taught to play the guitar by his cellmate - and a sensational new rock star is born.

programme.title: Rugby League Women's Challenge Cup
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors face St Helens in the final of the 2026 Women's Challenge Cup.

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra
programme.desc: Lavish, Oscar-winning account of the Russian royal family's demise in the days leading up to the 1917 revolution. When Tsar Nicholas weds the German princess Alexandra, the marriage proves unpopular with the Russian people, a situation not improved when she has four daughters. When she finally bears a son, the infant's acute haemophilia can only be controlled by the powers of the fanatical monk Rasputin.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: On a remote island in the Canadian Arctic, a pair of white gyrfalcons and a pack of Arctic wolves are both struggling to raise families during the short summer. As the falcons' eggs hatch, the race is on to catch enough young Arctic hares to feed them. The wolf pack are raising their cubs in a hillside den. The film records behaviour that has never been seen by scientists, let alone filmed. It is scored by the BBC's New TV Composers Scheme winner Anne Nikitin.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ickworth House, an Italianate palace in the Suffolk countryside, Paul Martin is joined by experts Adam Partridge and Will Axon, who try to help members of the public make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction. Will is impressed by a chinoiserie travelling clock found at the back of a wardrobe, but it's a campaign writing box that raises the roof over at the auction - and keeps the crowds in the saleroom entertained! Paul also meets the people trying to safeguard the future of the Suffolk Punch horse, which holds a unique place in the history of the county after which it was named.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a windswept island chapel battered by the North Sea to a quiet Welsh farmhouse that shaped the future of a language, this episode travels to two remote National Trust sites whose powerful stories have inspired centuries of pilgrims, revealing the delicate conservation work protecting their remarkable stories.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy explores how the battle for American independence wasn’t only fought on American soil but also unfolded in Britain itself, with sabotage, espionage and political unrest reshaping the empire. She reflects on a revolution that created an independent America but which also reshaped Britain and its monarchy.

programme.title: Boxing
programme.desc: Live boxing as Adam Azim makes his return from injury against Steve Claggett.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the songs performed on the show in 1993. Dance music and Euro pop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S. The early stages of Britpop begin to emerge with Suede and Radiohead, and Icelandic superstar Björk makes an early appearance. Then there are the big sellers from Meat Loaf and Gabrielle, and the grooves come courtesy of Jamiroquai, Chaka Demus & Pliers and many more.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Revel in many of the biggest singles of 1994 as the TOTP archives are opened once more. Love ballads proved irresistible in 1994, with Wet Wet Wet’s 15-week stay at number one with Love Is All Around and East 17’s Christmas classic Stay Another Day conquering the charts. Britpop bubbled up with Elastica and Blur, blockbuster hits reigned once more with Elton John’s Lion King theme, and a young Ant and Dec rapped their way to the charts. Plus, quirky and catchy hits from Shampoo, Whigfield and The Grid, a final TOTP performance by Kate Bush, and many more! Featuring Blur, Corona, Tori Amos, PJ and Duncan (Ant and Dec), Sheryl Crow, East 17, Whigfield, The Cranberries, Dawn Penn, Elastica, Wet Wet Wet, Shampoo, Elton John, Pretenders, Kylie Minogue, Youssou N'Dour ft Neneh Cherry, The Grid, Kate Bush, Bon Jovi, Crash Test Dummies and D:Ream.

programme.title: Everything Went Fine
programme.desc: A daughter is forced to reconcile her past with her father when he contacts her with a devastating final wish, in François Ozon’s powerful family drama. André has never been the easiest of fathers, but when he suffers a debilitating stroke and calls on his daughter Emmanuèle to help him die with some dignity, she finds herself faced with a painful decision. An adaptation of Emmanuèle Bernheim’s memoir, Everything Went Fine is both a detailed portrait of family relationships and a frank account of the process involved in assisted dying. In French with English subtitles.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the death of a chef in an arson attack on his restaurant, John and Janie come to a small fishing village to scrutinise the locals.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. Haunted by his own penalty miss with England, he inherits a broken team and a jaded fanbase. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team’s mindset and tackling his and England’s biggest fear – penalties.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Sammi Kinghorn explore the forests of the Welsh valleys at a critical time. As spring gives way to summer, wildlife is at its most active and visitor numbers rise, but wildfires pose a growing threat. They meet the people shaping the future of these landscapes, from fire prevention and recreation to conservation and training the next generation. Meanwhile, Tom Heap heads to Devon and Cornwall to investigate the state of the UK’s seaweed farming industry.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Some more unusual methods of growing are demonstrated at Beechgrove Garden. Ruth Vichos attempts to produce a mushroom crop and there’s a report on some of Scotland’s best bonsai, with everything you need to know to start that form of cultivation. Brian Cunningham is in the Seaside Garden at Beechgrove refreshing the planting with varieties that can withstand a coastal climate and Brian turns conifers into sculptures with topiary. Plus the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie visits Bee Edge Farm near Eyemouth to help open the barn doors and let the cows out for summer. Continuing to mark Landward’s 50th anniversary, Cammy catches up with a farming pioneer who was never one for sitting on the fence, and Rosie is stepping back in time in Aberdeenshire to find out what one old farm building can tell us about our agricultural past. Elsewhere, Leanna is on the trail of a walrus that has been turning up in harbours around Scotland's north coast.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Imad Alarnab brings a taste of Syria to the studio with his Middle Eastern feast – mujadara, pickled cucumbers, laban bikhiar and baba ganoush. Shivi Ramoutar has the perfect recipe for a fried fish taco, finished with a punchy green mango chow and tamarind pepper sauce. Patrick Williams serves the flavours of South Africa with his peri-peri poussin with confit potatoes, while Jacob Kenedy prepares two simple but tasty granitas. Pastry perfectionist Ravneet Gill rustles up a delightful summertime strawberry and custard tart, and Laura Smyth faces either her food heaven or her food hell. Meanwhile, Rick Stein takes to the shores of Cornwall to forage for razor clams, and the Hairy Bikers head to Tewkesbury to learn all about mustard balls.

programme.title: Athletics: Great Manchester Run
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 2026 Great Manchester Run.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Nigel Slater creates simple but delicious meals from ingredients that are often overlooked, including a lentil and chard stew.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Cook and Share
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry goes behind the scenes at a huge wedding, sharing memories of her own special day and cooking up some delicious dishes perfect for sharing at special occasions.

programme.title: Operation Crossbow
programme.desc: World War II drama. A small group of agents is sent on a vital mission to destroy a Nazi V-2 rocket base in Holland. Their aim is to prevent flying bombs from falling on London.

programme.title: The Searchers
programme.desc: Classic John Ford western. Believing the Comanche have abducted his young niece, embittered Ethan Edwards sets out to save her, accompanied by hot-headed Martin. But their long quest takes its toll.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team head to the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan to find one of the world’s most endangered big cats – the snow leopard. Kyrgyzstan sits at the heart of the global snow leopard population, but from the Himalayas in the south to Russia in the north, snow leopards are under threat right across their range. The goal of this expedition is to explore the remote and impenetrable Djangart valley. Snow leopards are known to live north and south of here, but nothing is known about what lies between. No one has studied the cats here because these high-altitude peaks are so difficult to access, but this region is packed with precious minerals, and mining companies now have their sights firmly set on the Djangart valley. Teaming up with Kyrgyz conservation group Snow Leopard Foundation, Steve and the team set out to find evidence of a breeding population of snow leopards. If they can find snow leopards living and breeding here, they hope to persuade the government of Kyrgyzstan to turn the Djangart valley into a protected nature reserve.

programme.title: Southbank at 75: You Are Here
programme.desc: An epic dance spectacular and electrifying journey through 75 years of British youth culture to mark Southbank Centre’s landmark birthday. This is a freewheeling journey of discovery across decades, remixing and reimagining some of the most vivid and influential youth and social movements that have driven culture forward since 1951, when the south bank of the Thames hosted the Festival of Britain.

programme.title: Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: Coverage from the 2026 Diamond League meeting at Rabat, Morocco.

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: In 2010, poorly 16-year-old Megan Bhari decides to launch a charity for seriously ill children. She’s battling a brain tumour, and knowing the toll illness can take, she decides to devote her life to creating fairy-tale experiences that might help children to forget, even briefly, that they’re sick. Believe in Magic becomes an immediate success. Inspired by Megan’s courage and self-sacrifice, celebrities and her beloved One Direction are more than willing to donate their time and money to the cause. And when her mother, Jean, posts news that Megan’s health has deteriorated and she needs expensive treatment in America, countless families and celebrity supporters rally around to support Meg. Together, they raise the money needed overnight. However, in an online chat, some parents whose own children have been treated for cancer abroad begin asking questions.

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes vocal powerhouse Jessie Ware, pop star Holly Humberstone, prolific producer Jack Antonoff, soul singer Samm Henshaw, electro duo Fcukers and director Tim Pope.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers
programme.desc: Jake Blues rejoins his brother Elwood after being released from prison, but the duo have just days to reunite their old R&B band and save the Catholic home where the two were raised, outrunning the police as they tear through Chicago.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special episode devoted to one of the most significant and consequential issues of our age: artificial intelligence. On the panel are: from the government, Darren Jones, chief secretary to the prime minister; Julia Lopez, shadow secretary of state for science, innovation and technology; Mo Gawdat, author, entrepreneur and former chief business officer at Google X; Laura Gilbert, senior director of AI at the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change; and Victor Riparbelli, founder and CEO of London-based AI company Synthesia.

programme.desc: When a chicken ends up inside Shaun’s pillowcase, Shaun tries to get it back, only for the chicken to end up in the farmhouse and the Farmer to think he is being haunted.

programme.desc: Callum challenges the bros to lead his giant rubber duck to water, as Adam decides to take Quakius Maximus to the skies. And both bros face the Gladiators referees.
